chunks.page_summary: This document provides instructions on how to create a correlation between laboratory and inline measurements using scaling coefficients in Rheonics sensors via the RCP Software.
product_names: Rheonics DVP Density and Viscosity Probe
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Scaling: How to create correlation between lab and inline measurements?
Modified on: Wed, 5 Feb, 2025 at 5:16 PM

What is the purpose of this article?
Explain the procedure to add scaling coefficients to Rheonics sensor readings using the RCP Software, to, for example, create correlations.

What products are involved?
Rheonics SRV viscometer, and SRD density and viscosity meter. (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)
Rheonics DVP Density and Viscosity Probe and DVM Density and Viscosity Module.

Scaling is an important tool when performing a calibration and/or correlating two or more values. Most of the time, a process measurement is compared against a known measurement (the standard) When you need to scale the Rheonics sensor measurements to match the standard or reference laboratory value, use the “Scaling” Rheonics Control Panel function.

Before adding any scaling factors to the readings, it is recommended that the user verify the correlation is possible between the Rheonics sensor and the external sensor. This is done by comparing and plotting readings for viscosity and/or density from both instruments and identifying a correct linear proportion. Multiple data points should be evaluated to ensure the scaled values will be valid for all operation ranges and not for a single point.

1. Scaling the measured channel
1.1. Open RCP and go to the “Scaling” tab (This requires to enable expert mode - find password required on RCP Manual). Once there, open the “Select Measurement” drop-down list and select the desired measure to scale.
key_facts: Scaling is an important tool for calibration and correlating two or more measurement values.
Rheonics sensor measurements can be scaled to match a standard or reference laboratory value using the 'Scaling' function in the Rheonics Control Panel.
Before applying scaling factors, users must verify the correlation between the Rheonics sensor and an external sensor by comparing and plotting viscosity and/or density readings.
It is crucial to identify a correct linear proportion for the correlation.
Multiple data points must be evaluated to ensure the validity of scaled values across all operation ranges, not just a single point.
To access the 'Scaling' tab in RCP, expert mode must be enabled, which requires a password found in the RCP Manual.
warnings_notes: To enable expert mode for the 'Scaling' tab in RCP, a password is required, which can be found in the RCP Manual.

Select the scaling method, “Single Point” for offset calibration should be used when either the measurements range is kept around a narrow value, or when the sensor response and the ideal response maintain the same behavior within the sensor operation range or “Two Point” for offset and slope scaling when the sensor works in a
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel version 5.3.1.6 includes an 'In-field Calibration Correction' feature within its 'Scale Output - Lab Correlations' section.
Users can select various measurements for calibration, including Viscosity, Density, Temperature, Kinematic Viscosity, Calculated Viscosity, Calculated Density, and Concentration.
When a measurement parameter is selected, the software automatically populates the unscaled and scaled values.
The panel supports two scaling methods: 'Single Point' for offset calibration and 'Two Point' for offset and slope scaling.
Single Point scaling is recommended when the measurement range is narrow or when the sensor's response consistently aligns with the ideal response within its operating range.
The 'Sensor Status' section includes indications for 'Air Check' and 'Sensor Clean', with a 'Clean' button for sensor maintenance.
The 'Scaling Menu' provides options to 'Save Scaling Record', 'Load Scaling Profile', view 'Scaling History', access 'Help', perform 'Field Calib. with STCM', and view a 'Tutorial'.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

Figure 3. Scaling methods

2. Single point calibration (Offset)

2.1. After selecting the "Single Point Scaling", click in the read button, this will populate the "Sensor Read Out" box with the raw value currently being measured by the sensor.

Alternatively, the user can write in the box a specific value of viscosity that was previously determined to create a correlation (i.e. the real-time value is not the only input possible). This holds true for both single and two point scaling.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software (version 5.3.1.6) includes a 'Scaling' section for 'In-field Calibration Correction'.
Calibration can be performed using 'Single Point Scaling' (for Offset) or 'Two Point Scaling' (for Slope).
For single point calibration, the user can populate the 'Sensor Read Out' box either by reading the current raw value from the sensor or by manually entering a previously determined viscosity value.
The ability to input a specific, pre-determined viscosity value for correlation applies to both single and two-point scaling methods.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel version 5.3.1.6 is utilized for in-field calibration correction.
The software provides options for both Single Point Scaling and Two Point Scaling.
The user interface displays unscaled/uncalibrated and scaled/calibrated viscosity values.
To save a read or specified value for Sample 1, the 'Set' button must be clicked.
Offset calibration requires entering an 'expected value' when the sensor output differs from the reference value.
The 'Write' button is used to store the expected value in SME (SRAM, MEEPROM, or other Non-Volatile Memory).
Scaling coefficients (C0 and C1) are displayed for Current, User (estimated), and Factory settings.
The Control Panel includes a Sensor Status section with options for Air Check, Liquid, and Sensor Clean.
The Scaling Menu allows saving and loading scaling records and profiles, and viewing scaling history.
Additional functions like 'Field Calib. with STCM' and 'Tutorial' are available.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

2.5. Read the Scaling Coefficients
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel version 5.3.1.6 offers an interface for in-field calibration correction using lab correlations.
Users can perform calibration using either Single Point Scaling or Two Point Scaling methods.
The calibration process involves obtaining a sensor read out and entering an expected value for the measurement, such as viscosity.
The software automatically calculates and displays scaling coefficients (C0 and C1) for current, user (estimated), and factory settings.
Clicking the 'SCALE' button uses stored samples to adjust the SME output, automatically showing a scaled value closest to the desired parameter.
After scaling with an expected value of 20 mPa.s, the software successfully changes the scaled/calibrated viscosity from 0.048493 mPa.s to 19.999 mPa.s.
The interface includes a 'Sensor Status' section for checking 'Air Check', 'Liquid' status, and initiating 'Sensor Clean'.
A 'Scaling Menu' provides options for 'Save Scaling Record', 'Load Scaling Profile', 'Scaling History', 'Help', 'Field Calib. with STCM', and 'Tutorial'.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the procedure for performing two-point calibration (slope calibration) using the Rheonics Control Panel software, detailing steps for inputting sensor readings and expected values for scaling.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. Two-point calibration (slope)
3.1. After selecting the "Two Point Scaling", click in the read button, this will populate the "Sensor Read Out" box with the correct raw value and save these values.
As for the single-point scaling, the user can directly write in the "Read Out" boxes specific values of viscosity that were previously determined to create a correlation and not use the "Read" button (i.e. the real-time value is not the only input possible).
Then click the "Set" button.

[Rheonics Control Panel 5.3.1.6 User Interface]

Figure 8. Saving Sample 1 by click-in set button in Two-point Scaling
key_facts: Two-point calibration is also referred to as 'slope' calibration.
When performing two-point scaling, users must click the 'Read' button to populate the 'Sensor Read Out' box with the correct raw value and then save these values using the 'Set' button.
For single-point scaling, users have the option to directly input specific, previously determined viscosity values into the 'Read Out' boxes, rather than using the 'Read' button for real-time values.
The Rheonics Control Panel displays both unscaled/uncalibrated and scaled/calibrated viscosity values simultaneously.
The software provides fields to view and manage scaling coefficients (C0 and C1) for current, user-estimated, and factory settings.
The interface includes a dedicated 'Scaling Menu' with options for saving/loading scaling profiles, viewing history, and accessing help or tutorials.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction
Figure 8. Saving Sample 1 by click-in set button in Two-point Scaling

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software (version 5.3.1.6) is utilized for in-field calibration correction, specifically for viscosity measurements.
Users must input expected reference values and click 'Write' buttons to save these 'LabValues' when performing two-point scaling.
Clicking the 'Scale' button triggers the SME to calculate and apply scaling coefficients, resulting in a scaled/calibrated output value.
An example demonstrates that applying scaling can multiply the actual viscosity value by a factor of 100x.
The software displays current, user-estimated, and factory scaling coefficients (C0 and C1).
The Control Panel provides features like loading/saving sensor states, viewing sensor status (Air Check, Liquid, Sensor Clean), and managing scaling profiles through a dedicated 'Scaling Menu'.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the scaling and calibration interface of the Rheonics Control Panel software, demonstrating both offset and slope calibration using a two-point method.

To enable expert mode: https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000289485-set-density-input-to-srv-inline-viscometer-for-dynamic-viscosity-and-kinematic-viscosity-outputs (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000289485-set-density-input-to-srv-inline-viscometer-for-dynamic-viscosity-and-kinematic-viscosity-outputs)
key_facts: The two-point calibration feature allows for both offset and slope calibration.
In the provided example, the scaled value is calculated as 84 times the viscosity with an offset of 20.
The Rheonics Control Panel version is 5.3.1.6.
The software provides options for Single Point Scaling and Two Point Scaling.
Users can set and write expected values for calibration points.
The interface displays current, user-estimated, and factory scaling coefficients (C0 and C1).
The system supports saving and loading scaling records and profiles, along with viewing scaling history.
The control panel includes features for checking sensor status (Air Check, Liquid) and cleaning the sensor.
